description: One of the oldest surviving imperial gardens in China, with the White Pagoda as its iconic structure.
A historic and cultural district in Beijing where traditional hutong scenery blends with lakeside views, offering enchanting nightscapes.
The highest point on Beijing's central axis, the best location to overlook the panoramic view of the Forbidden City, and a Ming dynasty imperial garden.
The main venue area of the 2008 Olympics, home to iconic structures such as the Bird's Nest and Water Cube.
A top-tier science and engineering university in China, known for its beautiful campus environment and strong academic atmosphere.
The central square of Beijing, the capital of the People's Republic of China, and the largest city square in the world.
A fashion landmark in Beijing, a hub for international brands, and a center for modern urban nightlife.
A distinctive artistic hutong in Beijing, featuring creative shops and cafes, offering an experience of old Beijing hutong culture.
A famous venue for crosstalk performances in Beijing, housed in a traditional theater building, providing an authentic crosstalk cultural experience.
One of the earliest zoos opened in China, renowned for housing rare animals such as giant pandas.
The Monument to the People's Heroes standing in the center of Tiananmen Square, a sacred memorial structure honoring revolutionary martyrs.
A cultural and leisure district in Beijing, gathering creative shops, cafes, and specialty restaurants.
The Olympic swimming competition venue, featuring a unique membrane structure, now transformed into the Ice Cube Ice Sports Center.
A commercial district with over 500 years of history, preserving the traditional hutong appearance and old Beijing commercial culture.
The main stadium of the 2008 Olympics, a masterpiece of modern architectural art and a symbol of the Olympic spirit.
A comprehensive exhibition of Beijing's history and culture, a modern museum with a rich collection of cultural relics.
The largest tomb in the Ming imperial mausoleum complex, the Changling Tomb, the burial site of Emperor Yongle Zhu Di.
The ruins of a Qing dynasty imperial garden, a historical witness to the 'Garden of Gardens,' and a patriotic education base.
